dataCard.title: Data Card Management
dataCard.type.title: Card Types
dataCard.type.name: Type Name
dataCard.type.nameTemplate.description: Template used to render the card's display name from its property values. Click a property chip below to insert it as {PropertyName}.
dataCard.type.nameTemplate.empty: No template configured. Cards will use their manually-entered name.
dataCard.type.delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this card type? All cards of this type will also be deleted.
dataCard.card.title: Cards
dataCard.card.name: Card Name
dataCard.card.delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this card?
dataCard.card.duplicate.message: A card with the same identity values already exists.
dataCard.matchRules.autoBindDisabled.too: Cards of this type will not be auto-associated with resources because no match property is selected.
dataCard.matchRules.autoBind.description: Pick which identity properties are used as match properties. A resource is auto-associated with a card when they share values on these properties.
dataCard.matchRules.autoBind.needsIdenti: Select at least one identity property above first.
dataCard.matchRules.autoBind.noMatchSele: No match property selected. Auto-association is disabled for this type.
dataCard.matchRules.autoBindEnabled.desc: When enabled, resources will be automatically associated with cards when they share matching property values. No manual binding is needed.
dataCard.matchRules.matchProperties.desc: Select which properties are used to determine if a resource and a card are related. When a resource has the same value as a card for any selected property, they become associated.
dataCard.matchRules.matchMode.any.descri: A resource matches a card if any one of the selected properties has the same value.
dataCard.matchRules.matchMode.all.descri: A resource matches a card only if all selected properties have the same values.
dataCard.matchRules.upsertRule.descripti:
When data arrives from external sources (enhancers, sync, etc.), the system looks up existing cards by the identity property values:
• Match found: other property values from the data are written into that card (requires "Allow auto-update").
• No match: a new card is created from the data (requires "Allow auto-create").

Example: an actor card type uses Name and Nationality as identity, plus a Birthdate property. When external data Name=A, Nationality=B, Birthdate=C arrives, the system finds the card with Name=A, Nationality=B and updates its Birthdate to C.
dataCard.matchRules.identityProperties.d: Properties used to uniquely identify a card. The system uses these to detect duplicate cards and decide whether to update an existing one or create a new one.
dataCard.matchRules.upsertRule.neitherHi: With neither option selected, external data won't create or update any cards.
dataCard.association.title: Associated Data Cards
dataCard.search.disabled.tooltip: This card has no match-property values, so no resources can be associated.
dataCard.search.confirm.title: Open in resource search
dataCard.search.confirm.content: A new search tab will be opened on the resource page using this card's match properties.
dataCard.validation.propertiesRequired: At least one property must be selected
dataCard.displayTemplate.description: Drag properties from the palette onto the grid, or move/resize placed properties. Properties not on the grid won't be displayed. Use the resize handle (bottom-right) to change size. Click the eye icon to toggle whether the property label is shown on the card.
dataCard.displayTemplate.hiddenSummary: The following properties may be hidden under certain conditions
dataCard.action.deleteAll.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all cards of this type? This action cannot be undone.
dataCard.action.createInitialData.descri: Automatically create cards based on existing property values in the system.
dataCard.action.createInitialData.onlyFr: Only create cards from values actually used by resources. For reference-type properties (choices, tags, etc.), the option pool may contain many values that are never used. Enable this to skip unused values.

If unchecked, all candidate values from each identity property will be used. With multiple identity properties, this generates a cartesian product, which may result in an unmanageable number of cards.
dataCard.action.createInitialData.allowN: For each identity property below, choose whether to allow empty values. If not allowed (default), combinations where this property has no value will be skipped.
dataCard.action.createInitialData.previe: Will create {{toCreate}} new card(s); {{alreadyExists}} already exist.
No new cards to create; submit is disabled.
